channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: What is ‘wellness governance’ (and why you should be practicing it)
item.description: It’s time we recognize the compelling case for “wellness governance.” Being a leader today requires a new level of performance. One that overrides fat...

item.title: AI Has Supercharged Fraud. Here Are 4 Ways Business Leaders Can Protect Their Companies
item.description: The CEO of an AI authentication company shares best practices for deepfake-proofing your business.

item.title: Bretton AI raises $75 million to use AI to combat financial crime
item.description: With new funding from Sapphire Ventures and Greylock Partners, the startup is upending anti-money laundering prevention at top financial firms.

item.title: The civil war inside American Airlines may not be all CEO Robert Isom’s fault
item.description: Also: All the news and watercooler chat from Fortune.

item.title: A Chewy Video Just Changed How I Treat My Cat. It’s a Masterful Lesson in Branding
item.description: Sharing your wisdom on YouTube can build customer loyalty. Here’s how.

item.title: America borrowed $43.5 billion a week in the first four months of the fiscal year, with debt interest on track to be over $1 trillion for 2026
item.description: "The longer lawmakers wait, the higher the price for Americans," warned Maya MacGuineas, president of the Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget.

item.title: This simple site makes it easy to track ICE’s actions
item.description: As the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigration continues, keeping up with Immigrations and Custom Enforcement can feel like navigating a maze....

item.title: Google Researchers Say These 12 Questions Indicate Whether You’re an Amazing Boss
item.description: How would Google say your leadership and management skills stack up?

item.title: Cognitive Science Says This 1 Belief Makes You Much Better at Coping With Change
item.description: It’s a crazy world out there. Scientist Maya Shankar says this shift in mindset can help you cope with so much change.

item.title: Why that $2 trillion software stock wipeout didn’t derail the AI bull market
item.description: 75% of companies in the S&P 500 have reported Q4 earnings, and profit per share across those companies is 12% higher than it was a year ago.

item.title: This CEO wants to do for hearing aids what she helped do for shapewear at Spanx
item.description: Laurie Ann Goldman, who helped turn Spanx into a global powerhouse, is now CEO of Audien Hearing.

item.title: Why ‘others have it harder’ is a form of empathy bypassing
item.description: When we minimize our suffering with statements like “I shouldn’t complain—others have it much harder than me,” it can seem evolved, empathetic, even w...

item.title: This new ad agency has a secret advantage, and it’s not what you think
item.description: At the new ad agency Ability Machine in Nashville, creatives have access to a full suite of tools ranging from podcasting and photography studios to l...

item.title: The worst part of work today is that nothing feels built to last
item.description: The legend of Sisyphus goes like this: As punishment for cheating death and embarrassing the gods, he is banished to the underworld and sentenced to p...

item.title: Hanky Panky, Maker of ‘The World’s Most Comfortable Thong,’ Sells To Private Equity After 48 Years
item.description: The co-founders of the iconic underwear brand tell Inc about the new owner’s plans to grow beyond lingerie and woo Gen Z.
